hero.title: Sustainable Progress & Equality Collective
hero.subtitle: A community-driven organization empowering people to learn skills, build careers, and create meaningful social impact through mentorship, applied research, and open collaboration.

story.heading: Born from crisis, built on shared purpose
story.p1: SPEC grew out of The Buffalo Project, a ten-year participatory research initiative that investigated how cultural data could improve learning programs. Led by Dr. Rhianna Rogers, the research revealed that students were not being adequately taught intercultural competencies, and that engaging communities directly could transform program design and delivery.
story.p2: When the COVID-19 pandemic hit in 2020, funding for that research was cut and students lost their positions overnight. Around the same time, Joe Torreggiani posted a public offer to provide mentoring, technical training, and small loans to people from underrepresented backgrounds. Rhianna reached out, and the two discovered an immediate shared passion for environmental and social justice. Joe offered to cover a student’s pay so the research could continue, and within a week they had formed SPEC as an open collective with full financial transparency from day one.
story.p3: We understand that for many people from underserved communities, giving back or helping others isn’t possible until they can access essential goods and services, learn professional skills, and achieve physical and financial security. That understanding drives everything we do: engaged research, collaborative mentorship, applied learning, capacity building, and pathways to sustainable careers.
story.p4: The name says it plainly. Sustainable progress means taking collective action to create a more equal and just world, without hurting people or the planet. That work continues.

approach.heading: The pillars of our work
approach.intro: Our approach integrates research, mentorship, learning, and organizational development to create lasting social impact.

pillars.title: Engaged Research
pillars.description: Prioritizing community participation in research including those affected as equal partners in shaping questions, making decisions, and creating programs.

pillars.title: Collaborative Mentorship
pillars.description: A inclusive and cyclical mentoring style that promotes fair learning, fostering balanced mentor-mentee bonds through open conversation and co-creation.

pillars.title: Applied Learning
pillars.description: Hands-on education where people use skills and theories in real situations, applying knowledge in actual projects for social impact.

pillars.title: Capacity Building
pillars.description: Empowering organizations through skill-building programs, leadership training, and community growth support.

pillars.title: Career Development
pillars.description: Connecting organizations with exceptional talent and skilled professionals, with ongoing leadership training and community growth support.

community.heading: The people who grew with SPEC
community.intro: Since 2020, career changers, recent grads, educators, and artists have come through SPEC to work on real projects and learn alongside each other. Some landed their first tech jobs within months. Others led research, curated art exhibitions, or designed courses on equity and inclusion.

stats.label: Raised and distributed to our community

partnerships.heading: Organizations we have worked alongside
partnerships.intro: SPEC believes in open collaboration across organizations. These partnerships have shaped our work and expanded what we can offer our community.

partners.name: RAND Center to Advance Racial Equity Policy
partners.description: RAND launched CAREP in 2020 to develop approaches and solutions that build racial equity in systems and policies. SPEC partnered with CAREP on a two-year digital education initiative, producing microcredentials, game-based learning tools, a digital expressions gallery, and the Journal of Engaged Research.

partners.name: BuildJustly
partners.description: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit working to empower the building of our country’s full economic potential through equitable technology. BuildJustly develops tools and research to advance digital inclusion and close the gap for the 1 in 3 U.S. households with no connected devices or low digital engagement.

partners.name: Open Source Medical Supplies
partners.description: A nonprofit that empowers people around the world to keep their communities safe through reliable open-source designs, manufacturing guides, and shared knowledge for medical supplies.

partners.name: Public Invention
partners.description: A nonprofit that invents in the public, for the public — building humanitarian open-source hardware including projects like the NASA-MCOG ceramic oxygen generator to help save lives in low-income countries.

connect.heading: Let’s talk
connect.intro: We love connecting with people who share our passion for sustainability, equity, and community-driven change. Whether you want to collaborate, partner, or just say hello — reach out.
